Over 150 new sources are now available in Data Connection - Inside the Product - Palantir Developer Community 

 Over 150 new sources are now available in Data Connection 

 Inside the Product 

 data-connection 

 krandoing 

 June 25, 2024, 7:08am

 1 

 Over 150 new sources are now available in Data Connection 

Our available source connections in the Data Connection application now include over 150 new supported source types . These new sources allow organizations to access data in even more locations using Palantir-provided JDBC drivers. New source types include DynamoDB, Dataverse, CosmosDB, LDAP, and many others.

 ns1 1179×1103 89.3 KB 

 Updated Data Connection landing page showing new connectors. 

The new sources have been released in the Experimental stage of Palantir’s development life cycle and are labeled as such. Each source will independently follow the stages of the development lifecycle as usage and support expand.

 ns2 921×1147 65.6 KB 

 Example source configuration with inline documentation. 

Learn more about connecting with Palantir-provided drivers , and explore the list of new available sources in our documentation.

 All third-party trademarks (including logos and icons) referenced remain the property of their respective owners. No affiliation or endorsement is implied.

Hi! Can you send a screenshot of the properties you’ve set for this connection? Feel free to redact any sensitive information from the screenshot. Also what is the behavior you are seeing? Are you getting any errors or is there just no rows showing up for any of the tables?

 image 1772×1854 282 KB

 Margot 

 October 26, 2025, 8:40pm

 5 

Has anyone got any experience with the Instagram connector? I have managed to create an app in Meta for developers and connect it to foundry, however the only insights I can successfully import are “comments” the others, of which there are many, are visible but then when I click on them to sync them to foundry I cannot see the actual data and get a token null error. There is only one token and it clearly works because I can see the comments, so I would assume the error is somewhere in the meta api but am stuck on how to fix it. Any advice?

 danielcdata 

 October 27, 2025, 3:02pm

 6 

Hey Margot, I suspect from the behavior you have described that there may be an issue with your Meta app causing permission errors. Can you share the exact error returned when you attempt to preview a table besides Comments? Additionally, if it loads, can you share a screenshot of the ‘Permissions’ table preview? This table returns the permissions available in the current session.

 Margot 

 October 27, 2025, 8:45pm

Hi @margot thank you for your post.

It sounds like this could be bug. So we can help you further, please file a Foundry Issue with your support team - this will be the best way to get this resolved.

Barth
